pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 75: socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_DGRAM) creates UDP socket; verifier.py confirms UDP packet reception

verifier.py lines 59-69: checks for 'is_binary' and 'is_json' flags. BrainFlow Streamer sends binary data (not JSON). Confirmed via web search: BrainFlow documentation and OpenBCI documentation show BrainFlow Streamer sends binary protocol, while Networking Widget uses JSON.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real EEG data from the PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset (eegmmidb 1.0.0), specifically Subject S001's eyes-open baseline recording (15,250 samples at 250 Hz sampling rate, ~61 seconds duration). The data comes from 8 EEG channels (Fp1, Fp2, C3, C4, P7, P8, O1, O2) recorded using an OpenBCI_V3 board. The task streams this recorded data via UDP to 127.0.0.1 on port 9000 using the BrainFlow Streamer (which transmits in binary format), requiring at least 50 UDP packets to be received for success. OpenBCI GUI and BrainFlow are real, production EEG systems used for neuroscience research.
